back_translation: Of David. Contend, O LORD, with those who contend with me; fight against those who fight against me.
Take up shield and buckler; arise and come to my aid.
Draw the spear and javelin against my pursuers; say to my soul, 'I am your salvation.'
May those who seek my life be put to shame and dishonored; may those who plot harm against me be turned back in disgrace.
May they be like chaff before the wind, with the angel of the LORD driving them away.
May their path be dark and slippery, with the angel of the LORD pursuing them.
For without cause they hid their net for me; without reason they dug a pit for my soul.
May ruin come upon them unawares; may the net they hid catch them; may they fall into that ruin.
Then my soul will rejoice in the LORD and delight in His salvation.
All my bones will say, 'O LORD, who is like You, who delivers the afflicted from one too strong for him, the poor and needy from the one who robs him?'
Malicious false witnesses rise up; they question me about things I do not know.
They repay me evil for good, leaving my soul desolate.
But when they were ill, I put on sackcloth; I humbled myself with fasting, and my prayer returned to my own bosom.
I went about as for my friend or my brother; I bowed down in grief like one mourning for his mother.
But when I stumbled, they gathered in glee; attackers I did not know gathered against me; they tore at me without ceasing.
Like ungodly men they mocked; they gnashed their teeth at me.
O Lord, how long will You look on? Rescue my life from their destruction, my precious life from these lions.
Then I will give You thanks in the great assembly; I will praise You among many people.
Let not my enemies who wrong me without cause gloat over me; let not those who hate me without reason wink the eye.
For they do not speak peace, but devise deceitful schemes against those who live quietly in the land.
They open their mouths wide against me and say, 'Aha, aha! Our eyes have seen it.'
O LORD, You have seen it; do not be silent. O Lord, do not be far from me.
Awake and rise to my defense, to my cause, my God and my Lord!
Vindicate me by Your righteousness, O LORD my God, and do not let them gloat over me.
Let them not say in their hearts, 'Aha, just what we wanted!' Let them not say, 'We have swallowed him up.'
May all who gloat in my distress be put to shame and confounded; may those who exalt themselves over me be clothed in shame and reproach.
May those who delight in my vindication shout for joy and be glad; may they always say, 'Exalted be the LORD, who delights in the well-being of His servant.'
Then my tongue will declare Your righteousness and Your praise all day long.
